session.user:
FIRST check docs/learned/index.md for existing documentation on this topic.
I need to find: 1. The existing test file for plan_header: `packages/erk-shared/tests/unit/gateway/github/metadata/test_plan_header.py` - read the last 100 lines to understand the pattern 2. The existing test file for update_objective_node: `tests/unit/cli/commands/exec/scripts/test_update_objective_node.py` - read the whole file to understand how tests are structured 3. The existing test file for check_cmd (if it exists): `tests/unit/cli/commands/objective/test_check_cmd.py` 4. The RemoteGitHub ABC to understand the `get_issue` method signature: look in `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/remote_github/abc.py`
Read all of these files and provide a complete summary of: - How plan_header tests are structured (test patterns used) - How update_objective_node tests are structured (fakes used, click testing patterns) - What methods RemoteGitHub has that are relevant (get_issue, update_issue_body, get_comment_by_id) - Whether test_check_cmd.py exists and what it contains
session.assistant: I'll help you find and analyze these test files and the RemoteGitHub ABC. Let me search for all of these systematically.

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Find the test files mentioned in the request

session.assistant: Now let me search for the plan_header test file:
Great! Now let me read all the relevant test files and the ABC:

1→"""Abstract interface for remote GitHub operations without a local git clone. 2→ 3→This gateway enables GitHub operations (branch creation, file commits, PR management, 4→workflow dispatch) using the GitHub REST API directly, without requiring a local 5→git repository. All methods take explicit owner/repo parameters. 6→""" 7→ 8→from abc import ABC, abstractmethod 9→ 10→from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.types import IssueInfo, IssueNotFound, PRReference 11→from erk_shared.gateway.remote_github.types import RemotePRInfo, RemotePRNotFound 12→ 13→ 14→class RemoteGitHub(ABC): 15→ """GitHub operations that don't require a local git clone. 16→ 17→ Every method takes explicit owner/repo parameters instead of repo_root: Path. 18→ Uses the GitHub REST API via HttpClient. 19→ """ 20→ 21→ @abstractmethod 22→ def get_authenticated_user(self) -> str: 23→ """Get the username of the authenticated GitHub user. 24→ 25→ Returns: 26→ GitHub username string 27→ """ 28→ ... 29→ 30→ @abstractmethod
... [392 lines omitted] ...
414→ Tuple of (is_authenticated, username, error_message)

20→objective-roadmap 21→ 22→```yaml 23→ 24→schema_version: '2' 25→steps: 26→ - id: '1.1' 27→ description: Set up project structure 28→ status: done 29→ pr: '#100' 30→ - id: '1.2' ... [894 lines omitted] ... 106→ assert result.exit_code == 0, f"Failed: {result.output}" 134→ assert result.exit_code == 0, f"Failed: {result.output}" 145→ """Error when step ID doesn't exist in roadmap.""" 159→ assert output["error"] == "node_not_found" 164→ """Error when issue doesn't exist.""" 177→ assert output["error"] == "issue_not_found" 182→ """Error when issue has no roadmap table.""" 196→ assert output["error"] == "no_roadmap" 211→ assert result.exit_code == 0, f"Failed: {result.output}" 234→ assert result.exit_code == 0, f"Failed: {result.output}" 277→ assert result.exit_code == 0, f"Failed: {result.output}" 297→ assert result.exit_code == 0, f"Failed: {result.output}" 318→def test_update_multiple_steps_partial_failure() -> None: 346→ replacement_failed path is defensive (parse_roadmap and 354→ {"node_id": "1.3", "success": False, "error": "replacement_failed"}, 366→ # AND semantics: success=false because step 1.3 failed 386→ assert result.exit_code == 0, f"Failed: {result.output}" 410→ assert result.exit_code == 0, f"Failed: {result.output}" 433→ assert result.exit_code == 0, f"Failed: {result.output}" 453→ assert result.exit_code == 0, f"Failed: {result.output}" 473→ assert result.exit_code == 0, f"Failed: {result.output}" 531→ assert result.exit_code == 0, f"Failed: {result.output}" 540→def test_include_body_on_failure() -> None: 541→ """updated_body field is absent on failure even with --include-body.""" 559→ """When issue body has no metadata block, returns no_roadmap error.""" 584→ assert output["error"] == "no_roadmap" 590→ 606→ 635→ 680→ assert result.exit_code == 0, f"Failed: {result.output}" 730→ assert result.exit_code == 0, f"Failed: {result.output}" 752→ assert result.exit_code == 0, f"Failed: {result.output}" 765→def test_neither_pr_nor_status_returns_error() -> None: 766→ """Omitting all update flags returns a no_update error.""" 780→ assert output["error"] == "no_update" 796→ assert result.exit_code == 0, f"Failed: {result.output}" 817→ assert result.exit_code == 0, f"Failed: {result.output}" 837→ assert result.exit_code == 0, f"Failed: {result.output}" 852→ 883→ assert result.exit_code == 0, f"Failed: {result.output}" 912→ assert result.exit_code == 0, f"Failed: {result.output}"

123→ with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="Missing required fields: schema_version"): 135→ with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="Missing required fields: created_at"): 147→ with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="Missing required fields: created_by"): 414→ with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="last_learn_session must not be empty"): 428→ with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="last_learn_at must not be empty"): 587→ """update_plan_header_learn_event raises ValueError if no plan-header block.""" 590→ with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="plan-header block not found"): 757→ with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="branch_name must not be empty"): 877→ """update_plan_header_worktree_and_branch raises ValueError if no plan-header.""" 880→ with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="plan-header block not found"): 1250→ """update_plan_header_remote_impl_event raises ValueError if no plan-header block.""" 1253→ with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="plan-header block not found"): 1303→ with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="learn_status must be one of"): 1460→ """update_plan_header_learn_status raises ValueError if no plan-header block.""" 1463→ with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="plan-header block not found"): 1551→ with pytest.raises(ValueError, match=msg): 1555→ with pytest.raises(ValueError, match=msg): 1642→ """update_plan_header_learn_result raises ValueError if no plan-header.""" 1645→ with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="plan-header block not found"): 1673→ """update_plan_header_learn_plan_completed raises ValueError if no plan-header.""" 1676→ with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="plan-header block not found"): 1730→ """update_plan_header_learn_materials_branch raises ValueError if no plan-header.""" 1733→ with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="plan-header block not found"): 1775→ """update_plan_header_ci_summary_comment_id raises ValueError if no plan-header.""" 1778→ with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="plan-header block not found"):
session.assistant:
Perfect! I've successfully read all the requested files. Let me provide you with a comprehensive summary.
## Summary of Test Files and RemoteGitHub ABC
### 1. Plan Header Tests (`test_update_plan_header.py`)
**File:** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/cli/commands/exec/scripts/test_update_plan_header.py`
**Test Patterns Used:** - Uses `CliRunner` from Click to test CLI commands - Dependency injection via `ErkContext.for_test()` with: - `FakeLocalGitHub` - local GitHub gateway fake - `FakeGitHubIssues` - issues gateway fake - `PlannedPRBackend` - plan store - `FakeTime` - time abstraction - Tests use helper function `_make_issue_with_plan_header()` to create test data - Tests use `format_plan_header_body_for_test()` from `test_utils.plan_helpers` to create test bodies - Tests use `find_metadata_block()` to verify metadata was updated - Validates output via `json.loads(result.output)` - commands return JSON - Assertions check both exit code and output structure
**Test Organization:** - Plain functions (no test classes) - Organized into logical sections with comments: - "Success Cases" (lines 50-157) - "Type Coercion" (lines 159-321) - "Schema Validation Errors" (lines 354-409) - "Input Validation Errors" (lines 443-487) - "Plan Not Found / No Header Block" (lines 490-544)
**Key Assertions:** - `result.exit_code == 0` for success, `== 1` for failure - JSON output with `success`, `plan_id`, `fields_updated` keys - Verify metadata blocks using `find_metadata_block(updated_pr.body, "plan-header")` - Check data types (int vs string preservation)
---
### 2. Update Objective Node Tests (`test_update_objective_node.py`)
**File:** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/cli/commands/exec/scripts/test_update_objective_node.py`
**Test Patterns:** - Same Click `CliRunner` pattern as above - Context injection: `ErkContext.for_test(github=FakeLocalGitHub(issues_gateway=fake_gh))` - Uses `ROADMAP_BODY_V2` constant for test data (lines 14-68) - Tests use `FakeGitHubIssues` with `updated_bodies` tracking for assertions - Tests also track `updated_comments` for v2 format (objective-header with comment updates) - Commands return structured JSON with `success`, `issue_number`, `node_id`, `previous_pr`, `new_pr`
**Key Test Behaviors:** 1. **Single node updates** - tests individual node modifications 2. **Multiple node updates** - tests batch updates with AND semantics (`success=false` if any node fails) 3. **Multi-step support** - tests `--node` flag used multiple times (lines 291-315) 4. **Explicit status setting** - `--status` flag overrides default status 5. **v2 format handling** - updates both issue body (frontmatter) and comment table (lines 659-693)
**Assertions Pattern:** - Exit code and JSON output - `assert output["success"] is True` or `False` - Check output fields: `issue_number`, `node_id`, `previous_pr`, `new_pr` - Verify mutations via `fake_gh.updated_bodies[0][1]` - the updated body text
---
### 3. Check Objective Tests (`test_check_cmd.py`)
**File:** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/cli/commands/objective/test_check_cmd.py`
**Test Patterns:** - Uses custom test context builder `_test_ctx()` (lines 43-54) - Injects both `FakeLocalGitHub` (local) AND `FakeRemoteGitHub` (remote) - `FakeRemoteGitHub` initialized with: - `issues` dict - `comments_by_id` dict for comment lookup - Tests use `VALID_OBJECTIVE_BODY` constant with metadata block (lines 80-130) - Supports both v1 (roadmap only) and v2 (objective-header + roadmap) formats
**Key Test Behaviors:** 1. **Validation checks** - multiple validation checks (Check 1-8): - Label presence, roadmap structure, status consistency, etc. - PR reference formatting (`#` prefix requirement) - Comment table sync with YAML 2. **JSON output structure** - returns phases, summary, next_node, all_complete 3. **Return types** - validates return types: `ObjectiveValidationSuccess` or `ObjectiveValidationError` 4. **Fan-out/fan-in support** - tests schema v3 with explicit `depends_on` (lines 765-854)
**Assertions:** - Exit codes for PASS/FAIL states - JSON output with `success`, `issue_number`, `phases`, `summary`, `validation_errors` - Text assertions checking for `[PASS]` and `[FAIL]` markers
---
### 4. RemoteGitHub ABC Methods (`remote_github/abc.py`)
**File:** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/remote_github/abc.py`
**Key Methods Relevant to Objective Plans:**
| Method | Signature | Purpose | |--------|-----------|---------| | `get_issue()` | `(owner, repo, number) -> IssueInfo \| IssueNotFound` | Fetch issue by number, returns issue or error type | | `update_issue_body()` | `(owner, repo, number, body) -> None` | Update issue body content | | `get_comment_by_id()` | `(owner, repo, comment_id) -> str` | Fetch comment body by comment ID | | `update_comment()` | `(owner, repo, comment_id, body) -> None` | Update comment body | | `add_issue_comment()` | `(owner, repo, issue_number, body) -> int` | Add comment, returns comment ID | | `get_issue_comments()` | `(owner, repo, number) -> list[str]` | Fetch all comment bodies for issue |
**Other Relevant Methods:** - `create_pull_request()` - create PR (used for plan PRs) - `update_pull_request_body()` - update PR body - `add_labels()` - add labels to issues/PRs - `close_issue()` / `close_pr()` - close issues/PRs - `get_pr()` - fetch PR-specific data (head/base ref names) - `get_prs_referencing_issue()` - find PRs that reference an issue
---
## Test File Summary Table
| File | Layer | Fakes Used | Pattern | Return Format | |------|-------|-----------|---------|---| | `test_update_plan_header.py` | Unit (CLI) | FakeLocalGitHub, FakeGitHubIssues | CliRunner + ErkContext | JSON with success, plan_id, fields_updated | | `test_update_objective_node.py` | Unit (CLI) | FakeLocalGitHub, FakeGitHubIssues | CliRunner + ErkContext | JSON with success, node_id, previous_pr, new_pr | | `test_check_cmd.py` | Unit (CLI) | FakeLocalGitHub, FakeRemoteGitHub | CliRunner + custom _test_ctx | JSON with success, phases, summary, validation_errors |
All three test files follow the same foundational pattern: 1. Create test data (issue with body) 2. Inject fakes into ErkContext 3. Use CliRunner.invoke() to run command 4. Assert exit code and JSON output 5. Verify mutations via fake state tracking
